what: FlyDSL stage1 kernel (t32x256x256) for bs=512/E=33 shapes instead of CK 4WG M128 stage1
why: FlyDSL has native stage1 fp4 kernels (tile_m=32,tile_n=256,tile_k=256) that may be better than CK ck2stages_gemm1. Create _flydsl_stage1_wrapper analogous to _flydsl_stage2_wrapper and inject via monkeypatch

what: FlyDSL stage2 t16x128x128_atomic for bs=512/E=257/d=256 instead of CK stage2 + NT=True
why: bs=512/E=257 uses CK stage2 with NT=True. FlyDSL stage2 with tile_m=16 has worked well for E=33 shapes. d_hidden=7168 is the N dimension for stage2 (down proj). Try FlyDSL atomic stage2 which may have better wave scheduling for the 257-expert shape.

DSV3 tuned CSV uses 4-WG stage1 for token>=64 with E=257. Currently using 1-WG (64x32x32x128_1x1) for bs=512/E=257. The 4-WG kernel provides 4x workgroup occupancy per CU which better utilizes the 256 CUs. Combined with FlyDSL stage2 from v143.

what: doweight_stage1=True to fuse routing weight multiplication into stage1 kernel
why: Currently doweight_stage1=False means routing weights are applied in stage2. Moving weight multiplication to stage1 can reduce stage2 work. The tuned CSV has doweight_stage1=0 but this may not be optimal for our shapes. CK stage1 kernel names have 'MulRoutedWeight0' (=False) vs 'MulRoutedWeight1' (=True).
